reviewer: agent matrix pass (headless Chrome, real ~223MB q8 download+run, first-visit + cached auto-init, light+dark, mobile+desktop)

rubric.evidence: REAL WASM run verified headless (fresh profile → 'Download model (~223 MB)' → click → download to 100% → ready): 6 sentences embedded via the feature-extraction pipeline; cosine matrix rendered with dim=768 (S1↔S2 0.61, S1↔S5 0.41), rowStatus='Done.'; semantic search 'How do I recover access to my locked account?' ranked '…Two-factor authentication…' 0.811 top. Console clean (0 errors). Also verified in Node with @huggingface/transformers@3.7.5: last_hidden_state [1,seq,768], related-pair cosine 0.733 > unrelated 0.563.
rubric.notes: NeoBERT class registered in transformers.js 3.7.5 (NeoBertModel/NeoBertForMaskedLM); onnx-community/NeoBERT-ONNX ships model_quantized.onnx (q8) so no version-pin needed.

rubric.evidence: Correct shape + semantics: the base encoder emits per-token 768-d vectors; the worker mean-pools (mask-aware) then L2-normalizes to a 768-d unit vector, so cosine is a dot product. Verified ranking related>unrelated and a sensible cosine matrix. Honestly framed: as a BASE encoder (not a contrastively fine-tuned sentence model) absolute cosines sit high/narrow — the page states this and directs attention to the ranking gap.

At-a-glance links onnx-community/NeoBERT-ONNX, states feature-extraction · WASM · q8 (model_quantized.onnx ~223 MB) and the real architecture (RoPE, SwiGLU, 28 layers × 768, 4,096-token context). Inference off the main thread in worker.js via lib/webai.js loadPipeline (pooling:mean, normalize:false). conformance: declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation + inference-off-main-thread pass.

Two-visit test in one Chrome profile: VISIT 1 shows 'Download model (~223 MB)', downloads, reaches ready; VISIT 2 (same profile) AUTO-INITS from the transformers.js cache with NO Download button — loader reads 'Model ready — running locally on your device. Clear cached model … 223 MB cached', a 'Clear cached model' control is present, and the matrix re-ran on auto-init with 0 console errors. The comparison encoders (GTE-ModernBERT, MiniLM) keep their own on-demand Download buttons.

Streamed download progress on first load (100%); Retry surfaced on failure by the shared loader; live #rowStatus / #cmpStatus role=status aria-live regions report 'Embedding…', 'Done.', and failures with an .err class; offline reuse via the transformers.js cache. Runs on the WASM path (no GPU) — headless no-GPU run produced real embeddings.

Sentences textarea, Embed & compare, Reset examples, semantic-search query input + Search (Enter-key), Inspect-passage select, Compare-three-encoders button, and the ladder controls (basics A/B similarity, practical search + dedup threshold slider, wild k-means k slider, multi-model three-loader ranking) all operable and keyboard-accessible. conformance: controls-operable=manual, buttons-have-names=pass.

Embeddings, matrix, PCA and search are all produced by the on-device pipeline run (real samples recorded above); the multi-model rung loads TWO more real on-device encoders (Alibaba-NLP/gte-modernbert-base and Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2) and ranks the same corpus with each. No canned result anywhere. conformance: no-fake-output=manual.

role=status aria-live #rowStatus/#cmpStatus + loader status; labelled textarea/inputs/sliders/select; sample chips are real buttons; the PCA scatter is an SVG with role=img + aria-label; the similarity matrix is a real <table> with scope'd headers and an sr-only caption. conformance: buttons-have-names=pass, images-have-alt=pass.

Desktop 1280 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 both render with no horizontal overflow (measured 0px on the main page), legible text, the at-a-glance table wraps, the matrix scrolls inside .matrix-scroll, the grid-2 collapses to one column, and the comparison table scrolls inside overflow-x:auto; light + dark screenshots reviewed.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified on both device classes (measured overflow 0px).

rubric.evidence: The forward pass runs in a Web Worker (lib/webai.js loadPipeline in worker.js); the only main-thread maths is cosine / a power-iteration PCA over a handful of already-computed 768-d vectors and a tiny k-means — all bounded. Main thread stays responsive; 0 console errors. Multi-model comparison batches the three encoders' forwards with Promise.all off-thread.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offload / break up heavy main-thread work; yield with scheduler.yield() past a 50ms budget; keep the interaction path clear.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference is off the main thread (worker.js runs the feature-extraction pipeline); the UI only renders returned vectors; the multi-model comparison uses Promise.all of off-thread runs. Client-side maths (cosine/PCA/k-means) operate on ≤ a handful of vectors, well under the 50ms budget.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless: matrix + search returned, page responsive, 0 console errors

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Avoid fixed widths that overflow narrow viewports; let content wrap; scroll wide content inside its own container.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the similarity matrix scrolls inside .matrix-scroll, the comparison table inside overflow-x:auto, the grid-2 collapses via auto-fit minmax, the at-a-glance table wraps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive check: measured 0px horizontal overflow at 360px; mobile screenshot reviewed

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Accessible form controls with clear, associated labels; don't rely on placeholder.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the sentences textarea, query input and range sliders all have associated <label>s; buttons carry text names.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce dynamic errors in an aria-live region on the page, not only the console.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live #rowStatus / #cmpStatus / #status report embedding failures with an .err class.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label controls; announce state in a live region; keyboard-operable; give non-text content a text alternative.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — labelled controls, live status regions, the PCA SVG has role=img + aria-label and the matrix is a real semantic table with a caption.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use modern flex/grid intrinsic sizing for responsive component layouts.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — grid-2 (auto-fit minmax) for the matrix/projection and loaders, arch-grid for the architecture cards, all collapsing cleanly on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: desktop + mobile screenshots reviewed, 0px overflow both classes

openQuestions: The base encoder's compressed cosine range makes the plain dedup threshold sit high (~0.9). A future rung could add mean-centering / whitening to spread the geometry for cleaner thresholds — additive, disclosed.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a long-context rung that exploits NeoBERT's 4,096-token window (embed a whole article vs a 512-truncated classic BERT) to show where the extended context actually changes the vector.
Add a WebGPU execution-provider fast path for neobert (honest WASM fallback retained) as an additive enhancement.

summary: Revision-1 agent matrix pass for neobert: real ~223 MB q8 download + on-device run verified in headless Chrome (first-visit 'Download model (~223 MB)' → 768-d cosine matrix + semantic search top 0.811 at 'Done.', console clean; cached auto-init with 'Clear cached model' and NO Download button, matrix re-ran on visit 2), plus the three-generation encoder comparison (NeoBERT vs GTE-ModernBERT vs MiniLM, all real on-device). Honestly framed as a next-gen BASE encoder (compressed cosine band; ranking carries the signal). Light+dark and mobile+desktop screenshots reviewed (0px overflow, matrix + table scroll in their own containers). Auto-checkable conformance assertions pass; manual-evidenced dimensions carry the agent verdict above.
